title: Performance, Migration, API Design, and Release Evidence · Mastery Check
summary: Migrate and publish a production-shaped TypeScript 7 library with measurable compiler performance and consumer proof. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a TypeScript 7 migration and release portfolio. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure cold and warm checks, project graphs, declarations, memory, and parallelism before changing configuration.
Migrate JavaScript incrementally with checkJs, JSDoc, allowJs, boundary validation, and shrinking suppression budgets.
Remove TypeScript 6 deprecations, compare TS6 and TS7 diagnostics, and document temporary API-tool compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 6. Competency probe: Verify clean install, strict build, tests, declarations, package exports, runtime smoke use, audit, rollback, and consumer acceptance.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for verify clean install, strict build, tests, declarations, package exports, runtime smoke use, audit, rollback, and consumer acceptance.
